claims.text: Broadcom reported fiscal Q2 2026 revenue of $22.187B, up 48% YoY, with AI semiconductor revenue of $10.8B, up 143% YoY.
Hock Tan said Q2 AI semiconductor bookings exceeded $30B against $10.8B shipped, a book-to-bill ratio above 2.8x, and that demand for XPUs and networking is 'simply insatiable'.
Broadcom guided Q3 FY26 revenue to $29.4B (+84% YoY) with AI semiconductor revenue of $16.0B (+200% YoY), and reaffirmed FY27 AI semiconductor revenue target exceeding $100B.
Broadcom's custom AI accelerator (XPU) customers include Google (TPU), Meta (MTIA), Anthropic, OpenAI, and two others; OpenAI's first XPU is expected in volume in 2027.

Broadcom, Apollo, and Blackstone announced the 'AI XPV Platform', a financing structure for up to 20GW of Anthropic's (and OpenAI's) Broadcom XPU chip purchases, with a first tranche of approximately $35B covering about 1GW, targeting 20GW by 2028.

Under the XPV structure Anthropic owns the chips and leases data-center capacity (issuing LOIs to developers); the first 1GW is sited at Fluidstack-managed sites in mid-2026, with Google backstopping Anthropic's lease obligations.

Google and Blackstone formed a TPU cloud joint venture with a $5B Blackstone equity commitment and a Bloomberg-reported total investment value of approximately $25B, targeting 500MW online in 2027, offering Google Cloud TPUs as compute-as-a-service.

CoreWeave's DDTL 4.0 (March 30, 2026) is an $8.5B delayed-draw term loan, rated A3/A(low), priced at SOFR+2.25% floating / ~5.9% fixed, syndicated by MUFG, Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Blackstone Credit & Insurance, and is backed by Meta contracts worth at least $19B (Bloomberg).

The CoreWeave DDTL uses a ring-fenced SPE architecture with a project-finance cash waterfall, 100% equity pledge, debt-service coverage ratio (DSCR) of at least 1.20x for sizing and 1.15x maintenance, collateralized by productive, power-secured, customer-accepted compute capacity.

Anthropic filed a confidential IPO S-1 with the SEC on June 1, 2026 at a reported valuation of approximately $965B, after closing a reported $65B Series H; Bloomberg reported an annualized revenue run rate of approximately $47B.
Anthropic's reported valuation rose from approximately $4.1B (2023) to $61.5B (March 2025), $183B (September 2025), $380B (February 2026), and approximately $965B (May 2026 Series H).

Apollo and Blackstone appear across multiple links of the AI compute financing chain: the XPV Platform (Anthropic/OpenAI XPU purchases), the Google-Blackstone TPU JV, and CoreWeave's DDTL 4.0 (Blackstone Credit & Insurance).
AVGO traded from approximately $293.70 in July 2025 to approximately $392.13 in June 2026, with an interim peak near $446.77 in May 2026.
OpenAI's custom-silicon program had a strategic partnership with Broadcom targeting a 10GW AI accelerator system with first rack deliveries in the second half of 2026.
Alphabet raised approximately $85B in an oversubscribed equity offering anchored by a $10B Berkshire Hathaway investment, earmarked for AI compute infrastructure.
